locked_parameters.observable_distribution: P is the observed law of (Y,D,Z,X), where D\in\{0,1\}, Z is an instrument or policy-shift index with finite support \mathcal Z, and X is a finite covariate cell.
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: \mathcal R_{\mathrm{lift}} consists of identified or bounded conditional potential-outcome marginals F_{a\mid z,x}, marginal sensitivity envelopes \mathcal M_{a,z,x}, dose/IV compatibility constraints \mathcal V, and local rank-copula tubes \mathcal C_{\rho,z,x}.
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: \mathsf B_{\mathrm{paste}}(d) is the lower/upper value of \Pr\{Y(1)-Y(0)\le d\} over a single full-data law Q satisfying all restrictions, not merely over separately feasible cellwise OT tables.
locked_parameters.verbatim_stage_minus1_block: This is a partial-identification proposal with tuple (\texttt{qid},\texttt{specialization},P,\theta,\mathcal R,\mathsf B)=(\texttt{pid\_ot\_dte\_marginal},\texttt{v1},P,F_\Delta,\mathcal R_{\mathrm{lift}},\mathsf B_{\mathrm{paste}}). Here P is the observed law of (Y,D,Z,X), where D\in\{0,1\}, Z is an instrument or policy-shift index with finite support \mathcal Z, and X is a finite covariate cell. The unobserved target is the distribution F_\Delta(d)=\Pr\{Y(1)-Y(0)\le d\}. The restrictions \mathcal R_{\mathrm{lift}} consist of identified or bounded conditional potential-outcome marginals F_{a\mid z,x}, marginal sensitivity envelopes \mathcal M_{a,z,x}, dose/IV compatibility constraints \mathcal V, and local rank-copula tubes \mathcal C_{\rho,z,x}. The bounding functional \mathsf B_{\mathrm{paste}}(d) is the lower/upper value of \Pr\{Y(1)-Y(0)\le d\} over a single full-data law Q satisfying all restrictions, not merely over separately feasible cellwise OT tables.

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite-overlap
named_assumptions.statement: \mathcal X, \mathcal Z, and the outcome support grid \mathcal Y are finite. Every cell (z,x) used in \mathcal R_{\mathrm{lift}} has P(Z=z,X=x)>0, and every admissible marginal \mu_{a,z,x} assigns mass only to \mathcal Y.

named_assumptions.label: ass:marginal-envelope
named_assumptions.statement: For each (a,z,x), the admissible set \mathcal M_{a,z,x}(P) is a nonempty closed convex polytope of probability vectors on \mathcal Y, and it contains every conditional potential-outcome marginal compatible with P and the stated marginal sensitivity model.

named_assumptions.label: ass:iv-dose
named_assumptions.statement: \mathcal V(P) is a finite collection of linear equality and inequality constraints linking the marginals \{\mu_{a,z,x}\} across z, x, and treatment states, representing the maintained IV or dose restrictions.

named_assumptions.label: ass:rank-tube
named_assumptions.statement: For each (z,x), \mathcal C_{\rho,z,x} is a closed convex set of couplings whose copula-rank score lies within radius \rho of a named baseline copula. The stability regime satisfies 0\le\rho\le\rho_{\max}(P), where \rho_{\max}(P) is the smallest nonzero slack in the active cellwise Frechet constraints. The classical unrestricted corner instead uses Assumption~\ref{ass:unrestricted-rho} and does not require \rho\le\rho_{\max}(P).

named_assumptions.label: ass:unrestricted-rho
named_assumptions.statement: There is a finite value \rho_{\mathrm{unres}}(P) such that \rho\ge\rho_{\mathrm{unres}}(P) implies \mathcal C_{\rho,z,x}=\Pi(\mu_{1,z,x},\mu_{0,z,x}) for every cell (z,x). The value \rho_{\max}(P) used away from the classical corner is only a stability upper bound and is not assumed to remove the copula restriction.

named_assumptions.label: ass:cycle-margin
named_assumptions.statement: The active cellwise OT dual solution at d is unique up to additive constants on each connected component of G(P), and every inactive inequality in \mathcal V(P) and \mathcal C_{\rho,z,x} has slack at least s_{\min}(P,d)>0. The comparison constant in Conjecture 1 is the computable value \kappa_*(P,d)=s_{\min}(P,d)/(4\max\{1,|\mathcal C(G(P))|\}).

estimand_functional: \Theta_L(P,d)=[\theta_L^\ell(P,d),\theta_L^u(P,d)] with endpoints given by finite cellwise OT programs over \mathcal L(P); \mathsf B_{\mathrm{paste}}(P,d)=\Theta_I(P,d) with endpoints optimized over one full-data law Q\in\mathcal Q(P).

conjectures.label: cycle-frontier
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite-overlap}--\ref{ass:cycle-margin}, the lifted OT relaxation is sharp for the DTE tail probability at \(d\),
\[
\Theta_I(P,d)=\Theta_L(P,d),
\]
if and only if the endpoint-normalized cycle-balance defect satisfies \(\Gamma(P,d)=0\).  Moreover, if \(\Gamma(P,d)>0\), then at least one endpoint of \(\Theta_L(P,d)\) differs from the corresponding sharp endpoint of \(\Theta_I(P,d)\) by at least \(\kappa_*(P,d)\Gamma(P,d)\).

conjectures.label: witness
conjectures.statement:
There exists an open set of finite-support laws with two instrument values, two covariate cells, and three outcome support points for which all local marginal envelopes and rank-copula tubes are nonempty, every cellwise OT subproblem has a strictly interior optimizer, and \(\Gamma(P,d)>0\) for \(d=0\).  One concrete center of the open set has equal cell weights \(P(Z=z,X=x)=1/4\), \(\mathcal Y=\{0,1,2\}\), and the following cellwise marginals for \((Y(1),Y(0))\):
\[
\begin{array}{c|cc}
(z,x) & \mu_{1,z,x} & \mu_{0,z,x}\\ \hline
(0,a) & (.42,.36,.22) & (.34,.40,.26)\\
(1,a) & (.35,.41,.24) & (.29,.45,.26)\\
(1,b) & (.31,.43,.26) & (.37,.35,.28)\\
(0,b) & (.39,.33,.28) & (.32,.38,.30)
\end{array}
\]
The active IV equalities store the two oriented contrasts \((0,a)\to(1,a)\) and \((0,b)\to(1,b)\), while the active rank-tube equalities store \((1,a)\to(1,b)\) and \((0,b)\to(0,a)\).  For the lexicographically normalized upper-endpoint duals at \(d=0\), the certificate for the cycle \(c:(0,a)\to(1,a)\to(1,b)\to(0,b)\to(0,a)\) is
\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c|c|c}
\text{cycle edge} & \text{stored arc} & r^u & \sigma & \sigma r^u\\ \hline
(0,a)\to(1,a) & (0,a)\to(1,a) & 0.06 & 1 & 0.06\\
(1,a)\to(1,b) & (1,a)\to(1,b) & 0.04 & 1 & 0.04\\
(1,b)\to(0,b) & (0,b)\to(1,b) & -0.03 & -1 & 0.03\\
(0,b)\to(0,a) & (0,b)\to(0,a) & 0.01 & 1 & 0.01
\end{array}
\]
Thus \(b_{c,u}(P,0)=0.06+0.04+0.03+0.01=0.14\).  The analogous lower-endpoint certificate has oriented contributions \(0.01,0.00,0.01,0.00\), so \(b_{c,\ell}(P,0)=0.02\), and therefore \(\Gamma(P,0)\ge0.14\).  On a small neighborhood preserving the active set and the residual signs, \(\Theta_L(P,0)\) strictly contains \(\Theta_I(P,0)\).

supporting_theorems.label: cellwise-outer
supporting_theorems.statement:
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite-overlap}--\ref{ass:rank-tube}, for every threshold \(d\),
\[
\Theta_I(P,d)\subseteq \Theta_L(P,d).
\]
Equivalently, optimizing \(\one\{y_1-y_0\le d\}\) over separately feasible cellwise couplings can only weakly expand the sharp identified interval obtained by optimizing over a single admissible full-data law.
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: lifted OT is a valid outer envelope]\label{thm:cellwise-outer}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite-overlap}--\ref{ass:rank-tube}, for every threshold \(d\),
\[
\Theta_I(P,d)\subseteq \Theta_L(P,d).
\]
Equivalently, optimizing \(\one\{y_1-y_0\le d\}\) over separately feasible cellwise couplings can only weakly expand the sharp identified interval obtained by optimizing over a single admissible full-data law.
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
Fix an admissible full-data law \(Q\in\mathcal Q(P)\).  By Assumption~\ref{ass:finite-overlap}, each used cell \(s=(z,x)\) has positive mass \(p_s=P(Z=z,X=x)\), so the conditional joint table \(\pi_s^Q=\mathcal L_Q(Y(1),Y(0)\mid Z=z,X=x)\) is well-defined on the finite grid \(\mathcal Y^2\).  Its coordinate marginals are \(\mu_{1,s}^Q\) and \(\mu_{0,s}^Q\), so \(\pi_s^Q\in\Pi(\mu_{1,s}^Q,\mu_{0,s}^Q)\) by the definition of conditional marginalization.  Because \(Q\) is admissible, Assumption~\ref{ass:marginal-envelope} puts every \(\mu_{a,s}^Q\) in \(\mathcal M_{a,s}(P)\), Assumption~\ref{ass:iv-dose} makes the family \(\{\mu_{a,s}^Q\}_{a,s}\) satisfy \(\mathcal V(P)\), and Assumption~\ref{ass:rank-tube} gives \(\pi_s^Q\in\mathcal C_{\rho,s}\) for every cell.  Hence the projected collection \(\{(\mu_{1,s}^Q,\mu_{0,s}^Q,\pi_s^Q):s\}\) belongs to \(\mathcal L(P)\).

For this projected lifted feasible point, the lifted objective equals the full-law tail probability by the law of total probability over the finite partition \((Z,X)=s\):
\[
Q\{Y(1)-Y(0)\le d\}=\sum_s p_s\sum_{y_1,y_0\in\mathcal Y}\one\{y_1-y_0\le d\}\pi_s^Q(y_1,y_0).
\]
Therefore every objective value attainable by an admissible full-data law is attainable by a lifted feasible collection.  Taking infima gives \(\theta_L^\ell(P,d)\le \theta_{\mathrm{paste}}^\ell(P,d)\), and taking suprema gives \(\theta_{\mathrm{paste}}^u(P,d)\le \theta_L^u(P,d)\).  Since \(\Theta_I(P,d)=[\theta_{\mathrm{paste}}^\ell(P,d),\theta_{\mathrm{paste}}^u(P,d)]\) and \(\Theta_L(P,d)=[\theta_L^\ell(P,d),\theta_L^u(P,d)]\), the interval inclusion follows.
\end{proof}

bypass_justified: Manski/IV primitives operate at a potential-outcome bound abstraction, while this artifact needs finite cellwise OT tables and cycle-pasting residuals.
No local-search hit exposed an existing finite OT/cycle-balance primitive, so the setup uses a finite algebraic formulation directly.
